headline: Sarawak Launches Own State Shipping Line With 8 Vessels
context: Sarawak launched Sarawak Marine Corporation in November 2025 with 8 cargo vessels to reduce dependency on Peninsula shipping companies.

cards.big: Sarawak Marine Corporation launched with 8 vessels November 2025
cards.sub: State-owned shipping line aims to cut cargo costs by 30% on internal routes

cards.big: Initial fleet of 8 vessels cost RM420 million funded from state reserves
cards.sub: Routes connect Kuching Sibu Bintulu and Miri with weekly scheduled services

cards.big: Freight rates on Kuching-Sibu route dropped 25% within 3 months of launch
cards.sub: Competing private operators filed complaints with Competition Commission

cards.big: Plan to expand to 15 vessels by 2028 serving Sabah and Brunei routes
cards.sub: State government aims to eventually replace cabotage-dependent shipping entirely

cards.big: State shipping tests whether Sarawak can build autonomy through commercial infrastructure
Eight state-owned vessels break Sabah's shipping dependency. Vertical integration is Sarawak's autonomy strategy.
